methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Scale AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Private), post-money $29B

data.narrative:
Baykar is a private Defense & Security company founded by Özdemir Bayraktar. The broader sector comprises 21 same-sector peers, including 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers such as Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Shield AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety. However, constructing a tight peer set proves difficult: only 1 company matches Baykar's stage within the Defense & Security universe, with zero adjacent-stage or similar-valuation-tier peers identified, falling short of the minimum 3 datapoints required for reliable peer-median triangulation.

Given these constraints, peer-median methodology is inapplicable. The insufficient narrow peer set—driven by the sparse intersection of stage, sector, and valuation tier—prevents meaningful multiple-based extrapolation. Expanding to all-sector Defense & Security comparables would introduce substantial comparability noise and obscure rather than clarify valuation.

The current rumored valuation of ~$10B cannot be validated or challenged using standard triangulation methods with the available data. Key uncertainties include Baykar's revenue scale, growth trajectory, and profitability profile relative to the identified peer universe. Absent disclosed financials or a sufficiently dense comparable set, quantitative valuation analysis remains inconclusive. Further diligence would require direct disclosure of revenue, EBITDA, and customer concentration metrics to enable meaningful benchmarking against public Defense & Security multiples or the private peer cohort.
